reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone AND in-page in headless Chrome — real coherent non-degenerate reasoning; RAG multi-model verified; desktop+mobile layout checked; page-load conformance clean), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (Transformers.js 3.7.5, WASM, fresh profile). Standalone: onnx-community/Qwen3-0.6B-ONNX at dtype q8 (model_quantized.onnx) produced coherent non-degenerate reasoning — 'Name three primary colors.' -> '<think> ... the primary colors are red, blue, and yellow ...'. In-page (CDP driver, fresh profile -> Download ~618 MB -> auto-init -> Send): the index answered a train-speed word problem with a full CORRECT reasoning chain (converting 45 min -> 0.75 h, speed = 60/0.75), 183 tokens at 5.6 tok/s on WASM, 0 console errors. NOT faked, NOT degenerate.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker; tokens stream via TextStreamer. dtype HONESTY: q4 ABORTS on the WASM EP (its GatherBlockQuantized op has no wasm kernel) and q4f16/fp16 are WebGPU-only, so q8 is shipped and this is disclosed in at-a-glance. WebGPU is an optional accelerator with an honest WASM fallback.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a chat completion; with thinking on it contains a <think>...</think> chain then the answer. The worker splits the two and the page renders the reasoning in a collapsible <details> and the answer below. Semantic sanity confirmed: correct arithmetic reasoning on the verified word problem.
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT demo: the smallest Qwen3 on the UNIVERSAL WASM/CPU path (no GPU), whereas the built Qwen3-1.7B (qwen3-webllm) is WebGPU-only. Hybrid thinking/non-thinking is exposed as a toggle.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/Qwen3-0.6B-ONNX, linked, plus the original Qwen/Qwen3-0.6B), the task (text-generation), architecture (Qwen3 decoder ~0.6B, 28 layers, GQA), the hybrid reasoning behaviour, quantisation (q8, model_quantized.onnx) WITH the q4-aborts / fp16-WebGPU-only disclosure, runtime/backend (Transformers.js / WebAssembly), size (~618 MB), and license (Apache-2.0).
rubric.notes: The 'How the API works' snippet shows the real chat_template_options.enable_thinking flag.

rubric.evidence: Every page loads its model through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'onnx-community/Qwen3-0.6B-ONNX', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:618}); a valid current on-device model auto-initialises with no Send-blocking Load click; only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.
rubric.notes: Verified: fresh profile -> Download (~618 MB) -> auto-init ready in the CDP driver.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/generating status is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the transcript is aria-live=polite; the shared loader exposes Retry on failure; a Stop button interrupts generation. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Generation failed: ...').
rubric.notes: No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere; conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: prompt textarea (Ctrl/Cmd+Enter sends), example chips (real buttons), Send/Stop, thinking-mode + sample checkboxes, temperature + max-tokens sliders, and the 'show next-token probabilities' button. Ladder adds a tool selector (practical), a run-both button (wild), and a notes/question RAG panel (multi-model).
rubric.notes: Ladder: basics (plain chat), practical (rewrite/summarise/grammar/formal/bullets toolbox), wild (thinking vs non-thinking side-by-side), multi-model (MiniLM retrieval -> Qwen3 answer, RAG).

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real Transformers.js text-generation pipeline; the streamed answer, the split reasoning chain, the token/latency readouts, and the top-12 next-token probabilities (one real forward pass -> softmax -> top-k) are all from real on-device runs. RAG answer is grounded in a MiniLM-retrieved note (verified: correct standup note retrieved + answered).

Every button exposes an accessible name (buttons-have-names pass); status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); the transcript/answer regions are aria-live=polite so streamed text is announced; sliders/checkboxes/selects/textareas are native labelled controls. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.
rubric.notes: The top-k probability bars carry numeric percentages (text), not colour-only.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + logical properties; the composer/controls wrap; the wild thinking/direct columns and RAG panels use auto-fit minmax(min(100%,...)). responsive-check measured 0px horizontal overflow at desktop (1280x800) and narrow mobile (360x740, DPR3), light+dark.
rubric.notes: Mobile download cost (~618 MB q8; +~25 MB MiniLM on the multi-model page) surfaced on the page. Composer/Send buttons meet ~44px min-block-size.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; tokens stream via TextStreamer callbacks (one postMessage per token) so the interface stays responsive during generation; a Stop control interrupts long generations. The extra top-k forward pass and RAG embedding/ranking also run off-thread.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — generation never blocks the interface. Measured 5.6 tok/s on WASM for 0.6B.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low, stream LLM tokens off the main thread in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all generation runs in a dedicated module Web Worker; tokens stream to the page via TextStreamer callbacks; the top-k and RAG embedding passes also run off-thread; a Stop control interrupts long runs.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js + mm-worker.js off-thread; UI responsive + Stop works during generation; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: schedule/stream incremental token work without janking the main thread
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Schedule tasks by priority so critical work runs first and background work is deferred.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — token DOM updates are small incremental textContent appends (not full re-renders); the heavy compute stays in the worker so the main thread only does lightweight paint work.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: per-token incremental append in the transcript; no long main-thread tasks observed

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive chat/control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — controls/composer flex-wrap; the transcript and columns use intrinsic sizing and auto-fit minmax(min(100%,...)).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px mobile+desktop overflow measured by responsive-check, light+dark

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region announcing streamed status and errors
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; make streamed content live; give every control an accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; transcript/answer are aria-live=polite; chips and buttons carry accessible names.

openQuestions: q8 is ~618 MB; a working WASM q4 (once GatherBlockQuantized gets a wasm kernel) would roughly halve the mobile download — worth re-checking onnxruntime-web releases.
Thinking mode can run long on hard prompts; should the wild/basics pages cap thinking tokens more aggressively on mobile to protect battery/latency?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a WebGPU fast-path demo variant (q4f16) as a NEW slug for capable devices, with an honest requiresWebGPU fallback to this WASM demo.
If onnxruntime-web ships a wasm GatherBlockQuantized kernel, add a q4 dtype toggle to cut the mobile download.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for qwen3-0-6b-wasm (onnx-community/Qwen3-0.6B-ONNX, text-generation, q8, ~618 MB — the SMALLEST Qwen3, a hybrid reasoning model, on the UNIVERSAL WASM/CPU path; DISTINCT from the built WebGPU-only Qwen3-1.7B). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome: standalone produced coherent non-degenerate reasoning; in-page the index answered a train-speed word problem with a full correct <think> chain (183 tokens, 5.6 tok/s, WASM, 0 console errors), and the multi-model RAG retrieved the correct note via MiniLM and answered from it. dtype HONESTY: q4 aborts on WASM (GatherBlockQuantized), fp16/q4f16 are WebGPU-only -> q8 shipped + disclosed. Off-main-thread worker + TextStreamer; real top-12 next-token 'see inside'; thinking/non-thinking toggle. Ladder: basics (chat), practical (rewrite/summarise toolbox), wild (thinking vs non-thinking), multi-model (MiniLM -> Qwen3 RAG). responsive-check 0px overflow desktop+mobile, light+dark. Apache-2.0.
